findings.title: Critical Performance Regression in Dashboard Load Time
findings.description: The recent deployment of v3.2 has caused a significant regression in dashboard load times due to unoptimized SVG rendering in the new charting library. This impacts user experience and could lead to customer dissatisfaction.
assumptions: The new charting library is the primary cause of the regression.

evidence.excerpt: Dashboard load time regressed from 1.2s to 4.8s after v3.2 deploy.

recommendations: Optimize SVG rendering in the charting library.
Consider rolling back to the previous version of the library if optimizations are not feasible.

findings.title: Database Migration Impact on API Response Times
findings.description: The migration from PostgreSQL 14 to 16 has introduced a regression in API response times due to a query planner issue. This needs to be addressed to restore performance levels.
assumptions: The query planner regression is the root cause of the API latency increase.

evidence.excerpt: API response times degraded 3x after database migration.

recommendations: Investigate and optimize the query planner settings.
Consider reverting to PostgreSQL 14 if the issue cannot be resolved quickly.

findings.title: Memory Leak in WebSocket Connections
findings.description: There is a memory leak in the WebSocket connections that leads to out-of-memory (OOM) crashes. This affects real-time monitoring capabilities and needs urgent attention.
assumptions: The memory leak is due to event listener accumulation in the metric aggregation pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: Real-time monitoring dashboard connections leak approximately 2MB per hour per connection.

recommendations: Profile and fix the memory leak in the WebSocket server.
Implement monitoring to catch OOM issues before they affect users.

findings.title: Implement Performance Regression Detection in CI Pipeline
findings.description: To prevent future regressions, it is recommended to implement a performance regression detection mechanism in the CI pipeline that benchmarks performance against a baseline.
assumptions: Automated performance benchmarking can effectively catch regressions.

evidence.excerpt: Implement performance regression detection in CI pipeline.

recommendations: Develop and integrate a performance benchmarking suite into the CI pipeline.

findings.title: Build Customer-Facing Status Page
findings.description: To improve customer visibility during incidents, it is recommended to build a public status page that displays real-time performance metrics and historical data.
assumptions: A status page will enhance customer trust and transparency.

evidence.excerpt: Build a public status page that shows real-time p50/p95/p99 latencies.

recommendations: Design and implement the customer-facing status page.

summary: The analysis identified critical performance regressions in the dashboard and API response times due to recent changes. Immediate actions are required to address memory leaks and optimize performance. Recommendations for implementing a CI performance regression detection and building a customer-facing status page have been proposed for future phases.
